title: Free the States Statement on OK House Bill 3700
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States publishes the memo it delivered to the Oklahoma House Public Health Committee opposing HB3700, a Texas-style heartbeat bill, while urging legislators to coauthor Rep. Mark Vancuren's HB4111 instead. The statement argues HB3700 violates God's law, dehumanizes preborn children without detectable heartbeats, denies equal protection, and orders state officials to abdicate their duty to punish murder.
audiences: Christian voter evaluating partial protection laws
Constitutional lawyer considering Fourteenth Amendment claims
Pastor teaching on the role of civil government

questions.question: What is the immediate legislative event Free the States is organizing around?
questions.answer: The House Public Health Committee was scheduled to hear HB3700, a Texas-style heartbeat bill, on Wednesday February 23, 2022, and Free the States urged Oklahomans to call and email each Republican member of the committee asking them to oppose HB3700 and coauthor Rep. Mark Vancuren's HB4111 instead.
questions.quote: On Wednesday, February 23, the House Public Health Committee will hear HB3700, a bill similar to the recent Texas heartbeat bill.

questions.question: Which Republican committee members did Free the States target for contact, and what action was requested?
questions.answer: Free the States listed eight Republican committee members by name with phone numbers and emails (Cynthia Roe, Toni Hasenbeck, Sherrie Conley, Dell Kerbs, Marcus McEntire, Carl Newton, Marilyn Stark, and Wendi Stearman) and asked constituents to request both opposition to HB3700 and that the legislators coauthor HB4111.
questions.quote: Please call and email each requesting their opposition to HB3700, and that they would coauthor HB4111 by Rep. Mark Vancuren.

questions.question: What does HB3700 actually do, according to Free the States' background section?
questions.answer: HB3700 would create civil liability for abortionists only when they have detected the child's heartbeat using standard medical practice; it leaves abortionists free of civil liability if they simply do not detect the heartbeat first, prohibits state enforcement, and exempts the mother from any of its prohibitions.
questions.quote: HB3700 would establish that abortionists are liable to civil lawsuit if they murder a child whose heartbeat they detected when testing for a heartbeat according to standard medical practice.

questions.question: Why does the memo argue HB3700 violates God's law?
questions.audience: Christian voter evaluating partial protection laws
questions.answer: Because Scripture commands governing officials to treat all human beings equally, the memo argues that protecting only those preborn children whose heartbeat happens to be detected by the killer shows partiality that God's word forbids.
questions.quote: God's word commands governing officials to treat all human beings equally. By only protecting children whose heartbeat is detected by the murderer, this bill shows partiality against those preborn children, which God's word prohibits.

questions.question: How does Free the States claim HB3700 dehumanizes some preborn children?
questions.audience: Christian voter evaluating partial protection laws
questions.answer: Because the law functions as a tutor for the public conscience, the discriminatory line drawn at a detectable heartbeat teaches society that preborn children without detectable heartbeats are less than fully human.
questions.quote: Because the law is a tutor, the partiality and discrimination contained in HB3700 serves to dehumanize preborn children without heartbeats.

questions.question: What is the constitutional objection to HB3700 spelled out in the memo?
questions.audience: Constitutional lawyer considering Fourteenth Amendment claims
questions.answer: By substituting a fine for the criminal protections that apply to born persons, and by exempting mothers who seek and authorize the killing, HB3700 fails to apply equal protection of the laws to preborn persons in violation of the Fourteenth Amendment.
questions.quote: this bill provides an exception to the prohibitions contained within it for the mother who seeks and authorizes the murder of her child. **By giving mothers a license to kill, this bill denies preborn children the equal protection of the law and harms abortion-minded mothers by not using the law to guide them away from murdering their child.**

questions.question: Why does the memo say the maternal-immunity provision perpetuates Roe?
questions.answer: By granting mothers immunity for authorizing the killing of their own children, HB3700 implicitly affirms the very Roe rationale that a woman has a right to terminate her pregnancy without consequence, even while purporting to restrict abortion.
questions.quote: this immunity for mothers who murder their children perpetuates the rationale of *Roe* by implicitly affirming the concept that a woman has a right to murder her own child with impunity.

questions.question: How does the memo frame the proper biblical role of civil government in relation to HB3700?
questions.audience: Pastor teaching on the role of civil government
questions.answer: Citing Romans 13, the memo says government's God-given role is to bear the sword against evildoers so that wrongdoers fear the consequences and citizens are restrained from evil, and HB3700 directly contradicts that duty by forbidding state enforcement.
questions.quote: the government is supposed to bear the sword against evildoers, that those who might do evil would fear the consequences and be better citizens.

questions.question: What founding-era authority does Silberman cite to support the state's positive duty to protect life?
questions.audience: Constitutional lawyer considering Fourteenth Amendment claims
questions.answer: He cites Thomas Jefferson's 1809 letter declaring that protecting human life and happiness, not destroying it, is the only legitimate object of good government, to argue Oklahoma must classify and treat abortion as murder.
questions.quote: As Thomas Jefferson said in an 1809 letter, **"The care of human life & happiness, & not their destruction, is the first and only legitimate object of good government."**

questions.question: What specific provision in HB3700 does Free the States cite as commanding state officials to abdicate their duty?
questions.answer: The memo points to language on page 6, lines 7-15 of the bill explicitly forbidding the state from taking or threatening enforcement of the act, calling that an explicit command for officials to refuse to uphold justice or treat abortion as murder.
questions.quote: HB3700 explicitly commands Oklahoma State officials to **not** uphold justice or treat abortion as murder: "No enforcement of this act…shall be taken or threatened by this state (P6, L7-15)."

related.title: Abolitionist Perspective on the Texas Heartbeat Bill: Three Quick Observations
related.reason: HB3700 is explicitly modeled on the Texas heartbeat bill this article evaluates from an abolitionist viewpoint.

related.title: Tennessee Fetal Heartbeat Bill—A Step in the Wrong Direction
related.reason: Provides a deeper case for why heartbeat-style bills like HB3700 are the wrong direction even when offered as restrictions on abortion.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Frames the broader abolitionist objection to partial measures of which HB3700 is a representative example.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: Distills the strategic disagreement that drives Free the States to oppose HB3700 in favor of HB4111.

related.title: The Holiness of God Destroys Pro-Life Incrementalism
related.reason: Develops the theological argument behind the memo's claim that HB3700 violates God's law by showing partiality.

related.title: Oklahomans for Life Asks Candidates to Oppose Abortion's Immediate Abolition
related.reason: Shows the Oklahoma political dynamic in which heartbeat-style bills like HB3700 compete with bills like HB4111 to abolish abortion outright.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Background on the Oklahoma legislative actors whose strategy of partial bills like HB3700 Free the States has been challenging for years.
